contract.name: v2/disaster/cfda/loans.md
contract.description: Returns CFDA-level disaster and emergency loan totals and program metadata filtered by required DEFC codes with optional keyword and loan type controls.

filter.description: Required filter wrapper with DEFC selections and optional refinements.

def_codes.description: List of Disaster Emergency Fund (DEFC) codes to include.
def_codes.constraints: minItems 1; values must match documented uppercase DEFC codes ['1','2','3','4','5','6','7','8','9','A','AAA','AAB','AAC','AAD','AAE','AAF','AAG','AAH','AAI','AAJ','AAK','AAL','B','C','D','E','F','G','H','I','J','K','L','M','N','O','P','Q','QQQ','R','S','T','U','V','W','X','Y','Z']; rejects non-arrays, empty arrays, lowercase codes, and values outside the list with 400/422 errors.

award_type_codes.constraints: minItems 1 when provided; allowed values ['07','08']; empty arrays return 422; values outside the set return 400; omitting the field leaves loan types unrestricted.

query.description: Optional case-insensitive keyword filter applied to CFDA metadata; null behaves as if omitted; whitespace-only strings are accepted but do not narrow results.
query.constraints: empty string returns 422; non-string inputs return 400; whitespace-only strings pass validation but act like the filter is missing.

page.constraints: minimum 1; numeric strings coerced; 0 or negatives return 422; decimals return 400.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows per page.
limit.constraints: minimum 1; maximum 100; numeric strings coerced; 0 returns 422; values above 100 return 422 with max message.

sort.constraints: allowed values ['award_count','description','code','id','obligation','outlay','face_value_of_loan']; requesting 'id' or 'code' triggers a warning and falls back to 'description'.

pagination.constraints: Defaults observed: page 1, limit 10, sort 'id', order 'desc' (with backend fallback to description when sorting by id/code).

spending_type.description: Optional spending metric flag; accepts any string or null with no observed effect on results.

totals.description: Aggregate totals across all matching CFDA programs.

award_count.description: Total disaster loan award count for the filtered set.

face_value_of_loan.description: Sum of face value amounts for matched loans.

obligation.description: Aggregate obligations for the filtered loans.

outlay.description: Aggregate outlays for the filtered loans.

results.description: Ordered CFDA program rows that satisfy the filters.

award_count.description: Number of disaster loan awards attributed to the program.

face_value_of_loan.description: Total face value of loans for the program.

resource_link.description: External reference URL for the program; may be null.

page_metadata.description: Pagination metadata for the current result page.

next.description: Next page number when more results exist; null on last page.

previous.description: Previous page number; null on first page.

hasNext.description: True when another page is available.

hasPrevious.description: True when a previous page exists.

messages.description: Optional warning strings (present when sorts fall back to 'description').

results.cfda_federal_agency: UNITED STATES INTERNATIONAL DEVELOPMENT FINANCE CORPORATION, UNITED STATES INTERNATIONAL DEVELOPMENT FINANCE CORPORATION
results.cfda_objectives: The U.S. International Development Finance Corporation (DFC) partners with
results.applicant_eligibility: The applicant must be a U.S. citizen, national, or

messages: Notice! API Request to sort on 'id' field isn't fully implemented. Results were actually sorted using 'description' field.

body.detail: Missing value: 'filter|def_codes' is a required field

quirks: Server emits a 'messages' array only when warnings exist (notably when requesting 'id' or 'code' sorts); supported sorts like 'description' omit the field.
Default requests attempt to sort by 'id', but the backend actually sorts by 'description' and reports the fallback when 'id' or 'code' is requested.
Numeric strings for pagination 'page' and 'limit' are coerced to integers, while decimal numbers are rejected with type errors.
'limit' cannot exceed 100; higher values return a 422 error with an explicit maximum message.
Whitespace-only keyword strings bypass filtering and behave as though the query is absent.
'spending_type' accepts any string or null value without changing totals or ordering.
risks: Callers relying on 'id' or 'code' ordering must handle the server fallback and accompanying warning message or sort client-side.
Aggregate award counts and financial totals can be very large; clients must handle high-precision numeric values.
Whitespace-only keyword inputs are accepted and skip filtering, so clients expecting strict validation should trim user input before sending.
gaps: Spending_type semantics remain undocumented; all tested inputs (including null) are ignored but no evidence shows a value that alters results.
cfda_website is documented as nullable but no probe observed a null value, so null-handling requirements are unclear.

API Request to sort on 'id' field isn't fully implemented. Results were actually sorted using 'description' field."]}

{"totals":{"obligation":99043.63,"outlay":99043.63,"face_value_of_loan":24486834.0,"award_count":1},"results":[{"id":4810,"code":"87.005"}],"messages":["Notice! API Request to sort on 'id' field isn't fully implemented. Results were actually sorted using 'description' field."]}
{"detail":"Invalid value in 'page'. '1.5' is not a valid type (integer)"}

mismatches: Docs specify Result.id as a string, but the API returns integer ids.
Docs imply 'id' and 'code' are fully supported sort fields; the API falls back to 'description' and emits warnings when those fields are requested.
Docs omit the 'messages' warning array, yet the API surfaces it when providing sort fallback notices.
gaps: Spending_type semantics remain undocumented; no observed value altered results.
cfda_website null-handling is unverified because all observed responses returned a string.
risks: Clients depending on 'id' or 'code' order must read 'messages' and handle fallback sorting behavior.
Large aggregate totals can exceed 32-bit ranges; callers should parse them as high-precision numbers.
Whitespace-only keyword submissions are accepted and skip filtering, which may surprise consumers expecting strict validation.
